reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is factually correct. At beacon-chain/verification/data_column.go:337-354, SidecarDescendsFromFinalized() only checks fc.HasNode(parentRoot), which verifies the parent exists in fork choice store. This does NOT verify ancestry from finalized checkpoint. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by broadcasting a data column sidecar with parent_root pointing to a block on a non-finalized fork (e.g., during reorg window before pruning). If the parent exists in fork choice but its ancestry conflicts with finalized checkpoint, gossip validation accepts it, causing honest nodes to propagate invalid sidecars and potentially reconstruct blocks that violate finalized chain ancestry. The fork choice store contains blocks on all viable forks until pruning at finalization (see doubly-linked-tree/store.go:200-241 prune() function). Spec reference (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json): 'Gossip validation must reject sidecar if finalized checkpoint is not an ancestor of block' with assertion 'forall sidecar: accepted(sidecar) => is_ancestor(finalized_checkpoint.root, sidecar.block_root)'. The spec requires get_checkpoint_block(store, parent_root, finalized_epoch) == finalized_root (see validate_data_column.go:137 comment and specrefs/functions.yml:4726-4731). Severity: Critical severity is justified under BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json severity_classification. This allows network-wide propagation of sidecars on finalized-conflicting forks, affecting consensus safety across all Prysm nodes (31% of CL diversity). Impact exceeds 'entire network' threshold for Critical.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Gossip validation must reject sidecar if finalized checkpoint is not an ancestor of block. (forall sidecar: accepted(sidecar) => is_ancestor(finalized_checkpoint.root, sidecar.block_root))
